package cmd

// TestWorkCommandHasPassthroughFlags verifies that ddx work exposes the
// harness/provider/model/min-power/max-power passthrough flags that operators
// use to constrain agent routing. These flags are forwarded opaquely; ddx work
// does not validate or branch on their string values.
func TestWorkCommandHasPassthroughFlags(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()

	workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
	require.NoError(t, err, "ddx work must exist")
	require.NotNil(t, workCmd)

	for _, name := range []string{"harness", "provider", "model", "min-power", "max-power"} {
		f := workCmd.Flags().Lookup(name)
		assert.NotNil(t, f, "ddx work must have --%s passthrough flag", name)
	}
}

func TestParseExecuteLoopFlags_AllFlagsPopulateSpec(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	setFlag := func(name, value string) {
		t.Helper()
		require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set(name, value))
	}
	setFlag("project", dir)
	setFlag("from", "HEAD~1")
	setFlag("harness", "claude")
	setFlag("model", "sonnet")
	setFlag("profile", "smart")
	setFlag("provider", "anthropic")
	setFlag("effort", "high")
	setFlag("watch", "true")
	setFlag("idle-interval", "45s")
	setFlag("json", "true")
	setFlag("local", "true")
	setFlag("no-review", "true")
	setFlag("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", "true")
	setFlag("review-harness", "codex")
	setFlag("review-model", "gpt-5.4")
	setFlag("max-cost", "12.5")
	setFlag("max-recovery-cost", "2.5")
	setFlag("preclaim-timeout", "45s")
	setFlag("request-timeout", "2m")
	setFlag("rate-limit-max-wait", "90s")
	setFlag("min-power", "7")
	setFlag("max-power", "8")

	spec, dispatch, err := parseExecuteLoopSpec(workCmd, true)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Equal(t, dir, spec.ProjectRoot)
	assert.Equal(t, "HEAD~1", spec.FromRev)
	assert.Equal(t, "claude", spec.Harness)
	assert.Equal(t, "sonnet", spec.Model)
	assert.Equal(t, "smart", spec.Profile)
	assert.Equal(t, "anthropic", spec.Provider)
	assert.Equal(t, "high", spec.Effort)
	assert.Equal(t, executeloop.ModeWatch, spec.Mode)
	assert.Equal(t, 45*time.Second, spec.IdleInterval.Duration)
	assert.True(t, spec.NoReview)
	assert.Equal(t, "codex", spec.ReviewHarness)
	assert.Equal(t, "gpt-5.4", spec.ReviewModel)
	assert.True(t, spec.OpaquePassthrough)
	assert.Equal(t, 12.5, spec.MaxCostUSD)
	assert.Equal(t, 2.5, spec.MaxRecoveryCostUSD)
	assert.Equal(t, 45*time.Second, spec.PreClaimTimeout.Duration)
	assert.Equal(t, 2*time.Minute, spec.RequestTimeout.Duration)
	assert.Equal(t, 90*time.Second, spec.RateLimitMaxWait.Duration)
	assert.Equal(t, 7, spec.MinPower)
	assert.Equal(t, 8, spec.MaxPower)
	assert.Equal(t, executeloop.SpecCurrentVersion, spec.SpecVersion)
	assert.Equal(t, "true", dispatch.JSON)
	assert.True(t, dispatch.Local)
}

func TestParseExecuteLoopSpec_RateLimitMaxWait(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []struct {
		name string
		set  string
		want time.Duration
	}{
		{name: "default", want: agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget},
		{name: "explicit positive", set: "90s", want: 90 * time.Second},
		{name: "zero uses runtime default", set: "0", want: 0},
		{name: "negative disables retry", set: "-1s", want: -1 * time.Second},
	}

	for _, tt := range tests {
		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			dir := t.TempDir()
			root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
			workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
			require.NoError(t, err)
			if tt.set != "" {
				require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("rate-limit-max-wait", tt.set))
			}

			spec, _, err := parseExecuteLoopSpec(workCmd, true)
			require.NoError(t, err)
			assert.Equal(t, tt.want, spec.RateLimitMaxWait.Duration)
		})
	}
}

func TestWorkZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauPolicyWithoutInitialMinPower(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
	stub.listPolicies, stub.listModels = canonicalFizeauPolicyFixture()
	stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
		ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"ready\",\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"readiness_checks\":[],\"score\":9,\"suggested_fixes\":[],\"waivers_applied\":[],\"recommended_action\":\"release_claim_retry\",\"suggested_amendments\":[],\"suggested_followup_beads\":[]}"}`)}
		close(ch)
		return ch, nil
	}

	dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "README.md"), []byte("# test\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "init", dir).Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.name", "Test User").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "add", ".").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "commit", "-m", "init").Run())
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
		ID:        "ddx-zero-config-work-powerClass-standard",
		Title:     "Work with inferred standard routing powerClass",
		IssueType: "bug",
	}))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(
		root,
		"work",
		"--once",
		"--project", dir,
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)

	requests := capturedImplementationRequests(stub)
	require.NotEmpty(t, requests, "ddx work must invoke implementation dispatch; output=%q err=%v", out, err)
	lastReq := requests[0]
	assert.Equal(t, "default", lastReq.Policy, "work should request the ordinary no-requirement Fizeau policy by metadata")
	assert.Equal(t, 0, lastReq.MinPower, "initial zero-config dispatch must not duplicate the selected policy floor as MinPower")
	assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Harness, "zero-config work must not hard-pin a harness")
	assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Provider, "zero-config work must not hard-pin a provider")
	assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Model, "zero-config work must not hard-pin a model")
}

func TestWorkZeroConfigStandardPolicyDoesNotDowngradeToCheapPolicy(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
	stub.listPolicies = []agentlib.PolicyInfo{
		{Name: "cheap", MinPower: 5, MaxPower: 5},
		{Name: "default", MinPower: 7, MaxPower: 8},
		{Name: "smart", MinPower: 9, MaxPower: 10},
	}
	stub.listModels = []agentlib.ModelInfo{
		{ID: "cheap-model", Power: 5, Available: true, AutoRoutable: true},
		{ID: "default-offline", Power: 7, Available: false, AutoRoutable: true},
		{ID: "smart-model", Power: 9, Available: true, AutoRoutable: true},
	}
	stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
		ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"ready\",\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"readiness_checks\":[],\"score\":9,\"suggested_fixes\":[],\"waivers_applied\":[],\"recommended_action\":\"release_claim_retry\",\"suggested_amendments\":[],\"suggested_followup_beads\":[]}"}`)}
		close(ch)
		return ch, nil
	}

	dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "README.md"), []byte("# test\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "init", dir).Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.name", "Test User").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "add", ".").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "commit", "-m", "init").Run())
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
		ID:          "ddx-zero-config-work-standard-no-cheap",
		Title:       "Work with standard powerClass and stale medium model snapshot",
		IssueType:   "bug",
		Description: "bug work should infer standard powerClass",
	}))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(
		root,
		"work",
		"--once",
		"--project", dir,
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)

	requests := capturedImplementationRequests(stub)
	require.NotEmpty(t, requests, "ddx work must invoke implementation dispatch; output=%q err=%v", out, err)
	lastReq := requests[0]
	assert.Equal(t, "default", lastReq.Policy, "standard-powerClass work must not downgrade to the weak policy when the model snapshot is stale")
	assert.Equal(t, 0, lastReq.MinPower, "initial zero-config dispatch must keep power as Fizeau policy metadata, not DDx hardcoded floor")
}

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod collections\\n                     WHERE name = $1\\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\\\",\\n                \u0026[\\n                    \u0026raw_collection,\\n                    \u0026key.namespace.database,\\n                    \u0026key.namespace.schema,\\n                    \u0026default_namespace.database,\\n                    \u0026default_namespace.schema,\\n                ],\\n            ))?;\\n        }\\n        Ok(())\\n    }\\n\\n    fn list_collections(\u0026self) -\u003e Result\u003cVec\u003cCollectionId\u003e, AxonError\u003e {\\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\\n            \\\"SELECT name FROM collections\\n                 ORDER BY database_name ASC, schema_name ASC, name ASC\\\",\\n            \u0026[],\\n        ))?;\\n\\n        Ok(rows\\n            .iter()\\n            .map(|row| {\\n                let name: String = row.get(\\\"name\\\");\\n                CollectionId::new(name)\\n            })\\n            .collect())\\n    }\\n\\n    fn collection_registered_in_namespace(\\n self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\\n///\\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if !exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"DROP DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\\n// Explicit verification path:\\n// container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\\n                    \\\"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\\\"\\n postgres_test_guard() -\u003e MutexGuard\u003c'static, ()\u003e {\\n        static GUARD: OnceLock\u003cMutex\u003c()\u003e\u003e = OnceLock::new();\\n        GUARD\\n            .get_or_init(|| TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: Grants {\\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\\n            }],\\n        },\\n    }\\n}\\n\\nfn make_storage() -\u003e User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    storage\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let storage: Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e =\\n        Box::new(make_storage());\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service call should succeed\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\\n28\\t/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\\n29\\tpub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -\u003e Response {\\n30\\t    let raw = uri.path();\\n31\\t    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\\n32\\t    let asset_path = raw\\n33\\t        .strip_prefix(\\\"/ui/\\\")\\n34\\t        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\\\"/ui\\\"))\\n35\\t        .unwrap_or(raw);\\n36\\t    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\\n37\\t        \\\"index.html\\\"\\n38\\t    } else {\\n39\\t        asset_path\\n40\\t    };\\n41\\t\\n42\\t    serve_asset(asset_path)\\n43\\t}\\n44\\t\\n45\\tfn serve_asset(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Response {\\n46\\t    match UiAssets::get(path) {\\n47\\t        Some(content) =\u003e {\\n48\\t            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\\n49\\t            Response::builder()\\n50\\t                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\\n51\\t                .body(Body::from(content.data))\\n52\\t                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\\n53\\t        }\\n54\\t        None =\u003e {\\n55\\t            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\\n56\\t            match UiAssets::get(\\\"index.html\\\") {\\n57\\t                Some(index) =\u003e Response::builder()\\n58\\t                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \\\"text/html; charset=utf-8\\\")\\n59\\t                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\\n60\\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\\n61\\t                None =\u003e StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\\n62\\t            }\\n63\\t        }\\n64\\t    }\\n65\\t}\\n66\\t\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"639a3f3a-e27d-4d23-b9e3-a0b9bc507e2b\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:45:23.541Z\",\"tool_use_result\":{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/src/embedded_ui.rs\",\"content\":\"//! {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = valid_claims();\\n    let token = issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\");\\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(\u0026token).expect(\\\"verify should succeed\\\");\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. data.text: I’ve got the shape now: a worker-only stale-source preflight belongs in `runWork` and `runTry`, not in the global semver gate. I’m reading the project-root resolver and command test helpers so I can build the regression tests around the real command path.

package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"sync/atomic"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type workResourceExhaustedChecker struct {
	calls       int32
	projectRoot string
	failOn      int32
}

func (c *workResourceExhaustedChecker) Check(ctx context.Context) (agent.ExecutionResourceCheckResult, error) {
	_ = ctx
	n := atomic.AddInt32(&c.calls, 1)
	failOn := c.failOn
	if failOn == 0 {
		failOn = 2
	}
	result := agent.ExecutionResourceCheckResult{
		ProjectRoot: c.projectRoot,
		TempRoot:    filepath.Join(c.projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "tmp"),
		EvidenceRoots: []string{
			filepath.Join(c.projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "executions"),
		},
	}
	if n == failOn {
		result.CleanupSummary = agent.ExecutionCleanupSummary{
			ProjectRoot:                 c.projectRoot,
			TempRoot:                    result.TempRoot,
			ScannedTempDirs:             1,
			RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs: 1,
			BytesReclaimed:              1024,
		}
		return result, &agent.ResourceExhaustedError{
			Detail: "temp root is full",
			Result: result,
		}
	}
	return result, nil
}

type panicAgentRunner struct {
	t *testing.T
}

func (r *panicAgentRunner) Run(opts agent.RunArgs) (*agent.Result, error) {
	switch opts.PromptSource {
	case "bead-lifecycle-intake":
		return &agent.Result{
			ExitCode: 0,
			Output:   `{"classification":"atomic","confidence":0.99,"reasoning":"single-slice"}`,
		}, nil
	case "bead-lifecycle-lint":
		return &agent.Result{
			ExitCode: 0,
			Output:   `{"score":9,"rationale":"ok","suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}`,
		}, nil
	}
	r.t.Fatalf("agent runner must not run after resource exhaustion: %+v", opts)
	return nil, nil
}

func TestWorkResourceExhausted_PrintsOperatorMessage(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	execTempRoot := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "ddx-exec-wt")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(execTempRoot, 0o755))
	t.Setenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR", execTempRoot)

	homeDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-home-")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	t.Cleanup(func() { _ = os.RemoveAll(homeDir) })
	agentCfgDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".config", "agent")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentCfgDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(agentCfgDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`providers:
  testprov:
    type: lmstudio
    base_url: http://127.0.0.1:1
    api_key: test
    model: test-model
default_provider: testprov
`), 0o644))
	t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
	t.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", filepath.Join(homeDir, ".config"))

	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "README.md"), []byte("# ddx\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: ./library
  repository:
    url: https://example.com/lib
    branch: main
`), 0o644))

	out, err := exec.Command("git", "init", projectRoot).CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "add", "-A").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "init").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))

	seedExecuteBead(t, projectRoot, &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "resource-bead-1",
		Title:     "First ready bead",
		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:  0,
		IssueType: "docs",
	})
	seedExecuteBead(t, projectRoot, &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "resource-bead-2",
		Title:     "Second ready bead",
		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:  0,
		IssueType: "docs",
	})

	checker := &workResourceExhaustedChecker{projectRoot: projectRoot}
	factory := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot)
	factory.resourceCheckerOverride = checker
	factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &panicAgentRunner{t: t}

	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	outStr, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--project", projectRoot)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, outStr, agent.ResourceExhaustedStopMessage)
	require.Equal(t, int32(2), atomic.LoadInt32(&checker.calls))

	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName))
	first, err := store.Get("resource-bead-1")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, first.Status)
	require.Empty(t, first.Owner)
	second, err := store.Get("resource-bead-2")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, second.Status)
	require.Empty(t, second.Owner)
}

func TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		_ = os.RemoveAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".codex"))
	})
	execTempRoot := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "ddx-exec-wt")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(execTempRoot, 0o755))
	t.Setenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR", execTempRoot)

	homeDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-home-")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	t.Cleanup(func() { _ = os.RemoveAll(homeDir) })
	agentCfgDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".config", "agent")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentCfgDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(agentCfgDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`providers:
  testprov:
    type: lmstudio
    base_url: http://127.0.0.1:1
    api_key: test
    model: test-model
default_provider: testprov
`), 0o644))
	t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
	t.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", filepath.Join(homeDir, ".config"))

	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "README.md"), []byte("# ddx\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: ./library
  repository:
    url: https://example.com/lib
    branch: main
`), 0o644))

	out, err := exec.Command("git", "init", projectRoot).CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "add", "-A").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
	out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "init").CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, string(out))

	seedExecuteBead(t, projectRoot, &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "resource-e2e-first",
		Title:     "First ready bead",
		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:  0,
		IssueType: "docs",
	})
	seedExecuteBead(t, projectRoot, &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "resource-e2e-second",
		Title:     "Second ready bead",
		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:  1,
		IssueType: "docs",
	})

	checker := &workResourceExhaustedChecker{projectRoot: projectRoot, failOn: 3}
	factory := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot)
	factory.resourceCheckerOverride = checker
	factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &panicAgentRunner{t: t}

	outStr, err := executeCommand(
		factory.NewRootCommand(),
		"work",
		"--project", projectRoot,
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, outStr, agent.ResourceExhaustedStopMessage)
	require.Equal(t, int32(3), atomic.LoadInt32(&checker.calls))

	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName))
	first, err := store.Get("resource-e2e-first")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, first.Status)
	require.Empty(t, first.Owner)

	second, err := store.Get("resource-e2e-second")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, second.Status)
	require.Empty(t, second.Owner)

	firstEvents, err := store.Events("resource-e2e-first")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotEmpty(t, firstEvents)
	var sawResourceExhausted bool
	for _, ev := range firstEvents {
		if ev.Kind == "resource-exhausted" {
			sawResourceExhausted = true
			break
		}
	}
	require.True(t, sawResourceExhausted, "first bead must record resource exhaustion evidence")
	secondEvents, err := store.Events("resource-e2e-second")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Empty(t, secondEvents)
}

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func seedLegacyLifecycleQueue(t *testing.T, workingDir string) {
	t.Helper()
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
	old := time.Now().UTC().Add(-time.Hour).Format(time.RFC3339)
	rows := strings.Join([]string{
		`{"id":"ddx-human","title":"human","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"` + old + `","updated_at":"` + old + `","labels":["needs_human"]}`,
		`{"id":"ddx-pseudo","title":"pseudo","status":"needs_investigation","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"` + old + `","updated_at":"` + old + `","work-last-detail":"rerun with smart agent"}`,
	}, "\n") + "\n"
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName, "beads.jsonl"), []byte(rows), 0o644))
}

func TestLifecycleQueueGate_BlocksWorkAndServerOnLegacyQueue(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	seedLegacyLifecycleQueue(t, workingDir)

	workFactory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	workOut, workErr := executeCommand(workFactory.NewRootCommand(), "work", "--once", "--json")
	require.Error(t, workErr)
	assert.Contains(t, workOut, bead.LifecycleMigrationGateCodeRequired)
	var workStatus bead.LifecycleMigrationGateStatus
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(workOut), &workStatus))
	assert.True(t, workStatus.Required())
	assert.Equal(t, 1, workStatus.LegacyNeedsHumanLabels)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, workStatus.LegacyNeedsInvestigationStatuses)

	tryFactory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	tryOut, tryErr := executeCommand(tryFactory.NewRootCommand(), "try", "ddx-human")
	require.Error(t, tryErr)
	assert.Contains(t, tryErr.Error(), bead.LifecycleMigrationGateCodeRequired)
	assert.Empty(t, tryOut)

	serverFactory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	serverStarted := false
	serverFactory.serverListenAndServeOverride = func(cert, key string) error {
		serverStarted = true
		return nil
	}
	serverOut, serverErr := executeCommand(serverFactory.NewRootCommand(), "server", "--tsnet=false")
	require.Error(t, serverErr)
	assert.False(t, serverStarted, "server RunE must not start on an unmigrated lifecycle queue")
	assert.Contains(t, serverErr.Error(), bead.LifecycleMigrationGateCodeRequired)
	assert.Contains(t, serverErr.Error(), "ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply")
	assert.Empty(t, serverOut)
}

func TestLifecycleQueueGate_AllowsMigrationDoctorHelpVersion(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	seedLegacyLifecycleQueue(t, workingDir)

	helpOut, helpErr := executeCommand(newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir).NewRootCommand(), "--help")
	require.NoError(t, helpErr)
	assert.Contains(t, helpOut, "Document-Driven Development")

	versionOut, versionErr := executeCommand(newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir).NewRootCommand(), "version")
	require.NoError(t, versionErr)
	assert.Contains(t, versionOut, "DDx")

	doctorOut, doctorErr := executeCommand(newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir).NewRootCommand(), "bead", "doctor", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, doctorErr)
	assert.NotContains(t, doctorOut, bead.LifecycleMigrationGateCodeRequired)

	migrateOut, migrateErr := executeCommand(newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir).NewRootCommand(), "bead", "migrate", "--lifecycle", "--dry-run", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, migrateErr)
	assert.Contains(t, migrateOut, `"legacy_needs_human_labels": 1`)
}

func TestLifecycleQueueGate_MigrateApplyClearsGate(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	seedLegacyLifecycleQueue(t, workingDir)

	beforeOut, beforeErr := executeCommand(newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir).NewRootCommand(), "bead", "ready", "--json")
	require.Error(t, beforeErr)
	assert.Contains(t, beforeOut, bead.LifecycleMigrationGateCodeRequired)

	applyOut, applyErr := executeCommand(newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir).NewRootCommand(), "bead", "migrate", "--lifecycle", "--apply", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, applyErr)
	assert.Contains(t, applyOut, `"marker_written": true`)

	afterOut, afterErr := executeCommand(newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir).NewRootCommand(), "bead", "ready", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, afterErr)
	assert.NotContains(t, afterOut, bead.LifecycleMigrationGateCodeRequired)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName, bead.LifecycleSchemaMarkerFile))
}

Plan for this bead:
1. Add a shared stale-source detector in `command_factory.go` that identifies “installed binary commit is an ancestor of source HEAD” for the resolved DDx source repo, and reuse it for `ddx version` warnings.
2. Gate `runWork` and `runTry` before any queue scan, claim, or attempt setup, with blocking text that includes project root, binary commit, source HEAD, and the recovery command.
3. Add focused regressions in `version_gate_test.go`, `work_test.go`, and `try_test.go` proving the warning path, the early worker failure path, and the “no claim / no dispatch / no attempt evidence” behavior.
4. Run the bead’s required `go test` subset and `lefthook run pre-commit`, then stage only the scoped files and commit once.

package cmd

import (

	agentlib "github.com/easel/fizeau"

)

type staleSourceRunnerProbe struct {
	t     *testing.T
	calls int
}

func (r *staleSourceRunnerProbe) Run(opts agent.RunArgs) (*agent.Result, error) {
	r.calls++
	r.t.Fatalf("agent runner must not run when stale-source preflight blocks: %+v", opts)
	return nil, nil
}

// TestWorkCommandHasPassthroughFlags verifies that ddx work exposes the
// harness/provider/model/min-power/max-power passthrough flags that operators
// use to constrain agent routing. These flags are forwarded opaquely; ddx work
// does not validate or branch on their string values.
func TestWorkCommandHasPassthroughFlags(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()

	workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
	require.NoError(t, err, "ddx work must exist")
	require.NotNil(t, workCmd)

	for _, name := range []string{"harness", "provider", "model", "min-power", "max-power"} {
		f := workCmd.Flags().Lookup(name)
		assert.NotNil(t, f, "ddx work must have --%s passthrough flag", name)
	}
}

func TestParseExecuteLoopFlags_AllFlagsPopulateSpec(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	setFlag := func(name, value string) {
		t.Helper()
		require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set(name, value))
	}
	setFlag("project", dir)
	setFlag("from", "HEAD~1")
	setFlag("harness", "claude")
	setFlag("model", "sonnet")
	setFlag("profile", "smart")
	setFlag("provider", "anthropic")
	setFlag("effort", "high")
	setFlag("watch", "true")
	setFlag("idle-interval", "45s")
	setFlag("json", "true")
	setFlag("local", "true")
	setFlag("no-review", "true")
	setFlag("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", "true")
	setFlag("review-harness", "codex")
	setFlag("review-model", "gpt-5.4")
	setFlag("max-cost", "12.5")
	setFlag("max-recovery-cost", "2.5")
	setFlag("preclaim-timeout", "45s")
	setFlag("request-timeout", "2m")
	setFlag("rate-limit-max-wait", "90s")
	setFlag("min-power", "7")
	setFlag("max-power", "8")

	spec, dispatch, err := parseExecuteLoopSpec(workCmd, true)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Equal(t, dir, spec.ProjectRoot)
	assert.Equal(t, "HEAD~1", spec.FromRev)
	assert.Equal(t, "claude", spec.Harness)
	assert.Equal(t, "sonnet", spec.Model)
	assert.Equal(t, "smart", spec.Profile)
	assert.Equal(t, "anthropic", spec.Provider)
	assert.Equal(t, "high", spec.Effort)
	assert.Equal(t, executeloop.ModeWatch, spec.Mode)
	assert.Equal(t, 45*time.Second, spec.IdleInterval.Duration)
	assert.True(t, spec.NoReview)
	assert.Equal(t, "codex", spec.ReviewHarness)
	assert.Equal(t, "gpt-5.4", spec.ReviewModel)
	assert.True(t, spec.OpaquePassthrough)
	assert.Equal(t, 12.5, spec.MaxCostUSD)
	assert.Equal(t, 2.5, spec.MaxRecoveryCostUSD)
	assert.Equal(t, 45*time.Second, spec.PreClaimTimeout.Duration)
	assert.Equal(t, 2*time.Minute, spec.RequestTimeout.Duration)
	assert.Equal(t, 90*time.Second, spec.RateLimitMaxWait.Duration)
	assert.Equal(t, 7, spec.MinPower)
	assert.Equal(t, 8, spec.MaxPower)
	assert.Equal(t, executeloop.SpecCurrentVersion, spec.SpecVersion)
	assert.Equal(t, "true", dispatch.JSON)
	assert.True(t, dispatch.Local)
}

func TestParseExecuteLoopSpec_RateLimitMaxWait(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []struct {
		name string
		set  string
		want time.Duration
	}{
		{name: "default", want: agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget},
		{name: "explicit positive", set: "90s", want: 90 * time.Second},
		{name: "zero uses runtime default", set: "0", want: 0},
		{name: "negative disables retry", set: "-1s", want: -1 * time.Second},
	}

	for _, tt := range tests {
		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			dir := t.TempDir()
			root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
			workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
			require.NoError(t, err)
			if tt.set != "" {
				require.NoError(t, workCmd.Flags().Set("rate-limit-max-wait", tt.set))
			}

			spec, _, err := parseExecuteLoopSpec(workCmd, true)
			require.NoError(t, err)
			assert.Equal(t, tt.want, spec.RateLimitMaxWait.Duration)
		})
	}
}

func TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	projectRoot, buildSHA, headSHA := seedStaleSourceCheckout(t)
	seedExecuteBead(t, projectRoot, &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "stale-work-bead",
		Title:     "stale source work bead",
		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:  0,
		IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
	})

	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
	stub.listPolicies, stub.listModels = canonicalFizeauPolicyFixture()
	runner := &staleSourceRunnerProbe{t: t}
	factory := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot)
	factory.Version = "0.9.0"
	factory.Commit = buildSHA
	factory.AgentRunnerOverride = runner

	out, err := executeCommand(
		factory.NewRootCommand(),
		"work",
		"--once",
		"--project", projectRoot,
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "ddx work: installed ddx binary is stale for this DDx source checkout.")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "project root: "+projectRoot)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "binary commit: "+buildSHA)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "source HEAD: "+headSHA)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "recovery: cd "+projectRoot+" && make install")
	assert.False(t, stub.executeCalled, "work must fail before any implementation dispatch")
	assert.Equal(t, 0, runner.calls, "work must fail before readiness hooks run")

	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName))
	got, getErr := store.Get("stale-work-bead")
	require.NoError(t, getErr)

+func (f *CommandFactory) warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd *cobra.Command) {
+	repoRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
+	staleness := f.detectInstalledBinaryBehindSource(repoRoot)
+	if staleness == nil {
+		return
+	}
+	_, _ = fmt.Fprint(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), staleness.warningMessage())

data.text: `lefthook` exposed one existing regression test that still asserted the older warning copy. I’m not changing scope; I’m updating the warning text in the implementation so it preserves the old phrasing and still carries the new project-root / commit details the bead requires.

package cmd

import (
	"bytes"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.git")
	initialSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")
	_ = gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "notes.txt", "stale\n", "stale")

	stdout, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", initialSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, stdout, "DDx v1.2.3")
	assert.Contains(t, stderr, "WARNING: installed ddx is built from")
	assert.Contains(t, stderr, "commits ahead")
	assert.Contains(t, stderr, "Run \"make install\" to refresh.")
}

func TestVersion_NoWarningWhenInSync(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.git")
	headSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")

	_, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", headSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Empty(t, stderr)
}

func TestVersion_NoWarningOutsideDDxRepo(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/example/not-ddx.git")
	initialSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")
	_ = gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "notes.txt", "stale\n", "stale")

	_, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", initialSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Empty(t, stderr)
}

func TestVersion_HandlesDetachedHead(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.git")
	initialSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")
	headSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "notes.txt", "detached\n", "detached")

	detach := exec.Command("git", "checkout", "--detach", headSHA)
	detach.Dir = repoDir
	detach.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
	out, err := detach.CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, "git checkout --detach: %s", string(out))

	stdout, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", initialSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, stdout, "DDx v1.2.3")
	assert.NotEmpty(t, stderr)
}

func initVersionTestRepo(t *testing.T, originURL string) string {
	t.Helper()

	repoDir := t.TempDir()
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "init")
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "config", "user.name", "Test User")
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "remote", "add", "origin", originURL)
	return repoDir
}

func gitCommitFile(t *testing.T, repoDir, relPath, content, commitMsg string) string {
	t.Helper()

	fullPath := filepath.Join(repoDir, relPath)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(fullPath), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(fullPath, []byte(content), 0o644))
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "add", relPath)
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "commit", "-m", commitMsg)
	return runVersionGitOutput(t, repoDir, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
}

func runVersionCommand(t *testing.T, workDir, version, commit string) (string, string, error) {
	t.Helper()

	factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
	factory.Version = version
	factory.Commit = commit
	factory.Date = "2026-05-05T00:00:00Z"

	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	root.SetArgs([]string{"version"})

	var stdoutBuf bytes.Buffer
	var stderrBuf bytes.Buffer
	root.SetOut(&stdoutBuf)
	root.SetErr(&stderrBuf)

	err := root.Execute()
	return stdoutBuf.String(), stderrBuf.String(), err
}

data.text: The commit is waiting on the same staged hook chain that already passed once. I’m just letting the commit finish; no more edits unless the hook returns something new.

Implemented the stale-source guard for `ddx work` and `ddx try`. The shared detection lives in `cli/cmd/command_factory.go`, `runWork` and `runTry` now fail before queue scan or claim when the installed binary commit is behind the DDx source checkout `HEAD`, and the error text includes the project root, binary commit, source `HEAD`, and `cd <root> && make install`. `ddx version` still warns instead of blocking, but now carries the same structured detail.

Added regressions in `cli/cmd/version_gate_test.go`, `cli/cmd/work_test.go`, and `cli/cmd/try_test.go` covering the warning path and the early-failure/no-claim/no-dispatch behavior. Verified with `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan|TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim|TestVersionWarnsStaleSourceBinary|TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr|TestCheckVersionGate' -count=1` and `lefthook run pre-commit`. Commit: `9011785f9` (`fix(cli): fail fast on stale source checkout binary [ddx-08b452ee]`).
